methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage company in Housing & Construction that has raised $5.5M to date. The sector comprises a limited peer universe of three private companies (ICON, BotBuilt, American Housing Corporation) and no public comparables, reflecting the nascent state of frontier construction technology.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression. Using total capital raised ($5.5M, rounded to $6M as a proxy for seed-stage value) and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $14M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Housing & Construction sector; the available universe lacks the minimum three datapoints needed for reliable triangulation at comparable valuation tiers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $12M (low) to $16M (median $14M). This range reflects standard seed-stage step-up mechanics but carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of direct peer benchmarking. Key risks to valuation include execution risk typical of early-stage hardware-adjacent companies and the early maturity of frontier construction technology markets. The median estimate of $14M should be treated as indicative pending stronger peer comparability or operational milestones.
